behaviors.description: A request whose signature does not match its body is rejected with a 401 response; the body is not forwarded.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is handler wiring across HTTP request parsing/raw-body bytes, signature verifier, and queue handoff (MOCK-MISS: cross-module handoff). A pure unit test of the verifier would miss miswired raw-body capture. Rejected System because behavior text only requires `401` + `not forwarded`, not real network/OS timing.
behaviors.test_description: Run the handler with an in-process HTTP test harness using real middleware stack, send body+bad `X-Signature-256`, assert `401` and assert queue write method was not invoked.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test depends on module interaction (request body bytes -> verifier -> enqueue gate), so Unit would be too low.

behaviors.description: An event is written to the persistent queue before the HTTP 200 acknowledgment is returned to the sender.
behaviors.justification: This is ordering across handler and persistence module; needs real local queue persistence path (DB/filesystem) and response sequencing. Rejected System because behavior text is about ordering, not real network/kernel behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Use in-process server + real test queue backend (local DB/file). Instrument queue write completion and response send timestamp/order; assert write commits before `200` is emitted.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: The description explicitly checks cross-module order (`written ... before ... returned`), which requires integration wiring.

behaviors.description: The handler returns the HTTP response without waiting for downstream processing of the event to finish.
behaviors.justification: Behavior targets decoupling between ingress handler and async processor; real failure appears at module boundary (enqueue/dispatch vs processor completion). Rejected System because real subprocess/OS/network is not required by the behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Wire handler to a downstream processor stub that blocks for several seconds after dequeue. Send valid event, assert HTTP response returns immediately while processor completion happens later.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test must exercise async handoff between modules, not just one function.

behaviors.description: The response arrives within 3 seconds wall-clock time even when downstream processing of earlier events is slow and concurrent.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly depends on wall-clock latency under concurrency; real scheduler/event-loop/backpressure effects can be hidden by in-process fakes (REAL-INFRA and MOCK-HIDE). Rejected Integration because wording requires `within 3 seconds wall-clock` under concurrent load, which is an OS/runtime behavior boundary.
behaviors.test_description: Start real Node process and send concurrent HTTP requests while earlier events trigger intentionally slow downstream work. Measure end-to-end response latency with real clock; assert each response is <3s.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: A lower-level mock-timer test could pass while real runtime contention fails the 3s SLO.

behaviors.description: An event whose ID was already received is not written to the queue a second time.
behaviors.justification: Dedup correctness is a state handoff between ingress logic and persistent queue/idempotency store. Unit logic alone can miss persistence/wiring races. Rejected System because behavior text is about duplicate-write prevention, not real external network/OS primitives.
behaviors.test_description: Using real queue persistence in test mode, submit same event ID twice through handler path; assert first write succeeds and second attempt does not create another queued record.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Requires cross-module state persistence to catch real duplicate-write failures.

behaviors.description: When the queue reports it is full, the handler returns 503 with a Retry-After header.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is translation of queue-full signal to HTTP status/header across module boundary. No real OS/network/LLM dependency; deterministic infrastructure behavior. Rejected System because text says `when the queue reports it is full`, which can be reproduced in-process.
behaviors.test_description: Configure queue module (or test backend) to return a `full` error on enqueue; call handler and assert HTTP `503` and `Retry-After` header presence/value.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Needs handler+queue wiring; a pure unit of one helper would be insufficient.

behaviors.description: The persistent queue actually enforces its configured capacity limit and rejects events that would exceed it.
behaviors.justification: This behavior is the queue component plus its persistence layer enforcing capacity. Real failure boundary is module+local storage interaction. Rejected Unit because `persistent queue` implies more than one local function. Rejected System because this can be validated with local DB/filesystem integration without external OS/network dependencies beyond normal local process I/O.
behaviors.test_description: Instantiate queue with real test persistence backend and capacity N, enqueue N events, then enqueue one more and assert explicit capacity rejection plus no extra persisted record.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test directly exercises queue+storage integration where capacity bugs appear.
